source_pr.title: planner: add more test cases for list partitioning
source_pr.body:
<!-- Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: close #xxx <!-- REMOVE this line if no issue to close -->

Problem Summary: planner: add more test cases for list partitioning

### What is changed and how it works?

No logical change, just add more test cases for list partitioning.

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test

### Release note <!-- bugfixes or new feature need a release note -->
None

symptoms: + c.Skip("skip race test") | rs := tk.MustQuery(fmt.Sprintf(`select * from tnormal %v order by %v limit %v`, randCond, orderCol, limitNum)).Sort() (planner/core/integration_partition_test.go)
failure_signature: + c.Skip("skip race test") | rs := tk.MustQuery(fmt.Sprintf(`select * from tnormal %v order by %v limit %v`, randCond, orderCol, limitNum)).Sort() (planner/core/integration_partition_test.go)
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it asserted an exact plan/EXPLAIN output that can vary between runs. Exact plan/EXPLAIN assertions are brittle when optimizer choices can vary. This PR stabilizes it by: + c.Skip("skip race test") | rs := tk.MustQuery(fmt.Sprintf(`select * from tnormal %v order by %v limit %v`, randCond, orderCol, limitNum)).Sort() (planner/core/integration_partition_test.go).
fix_pattern: + c.Skip("skip race test") | rs := tk.MustQuery(fmt.Sprintf(`select * from tnormal %v order by %v limit %v`, randCond, orderCol, limitNum)).Sort() (planner/core/integration_partition_test.go)
